more: n. m. S. घुण 1. (Sant. hutī ) A weevil. 2. The dust or powder of decayed wood or grain. 3. ( Met .) Grief; sorrow. ghun'nā, ghun lagnā, ghun jānā , v. n. 1. To be eaten or destroyed (as wood). 1. Khetvā meṅ ghunelā rahrī ke khuṅṭiyā, charūī meṅ ghunelā pisān, Choliyā meṅ ghune lā bālā re jobanā, mūrakh piyā agiyān . Bhoj. song. Yeh umr jise tum samjhe ho yeh har dam tan ko chuntī hai, Jis lakṛī ke bal baiṭhe ho din rāt yeh lakṛī ghuntī hai . Nazīr. This life on which you count consumes you bit by bit, Each moment eaten is the branch on which you sit. 2. To be the prey of consuming disease. Bhalī chaṅgī thī maiṅ, ghun lag gayā, haë haë! javānī meṅ, Nigoṛe chīre-vāle, nauj khāūṅ maiṅ davā terī! Wom. Rāhat. In blooming health the canker worm infects my youth, Nev…

def: s.m. Complaint; lamentation; reproach, blame; accusation; remonstrance
more: s. m. Complaint, lamentation, reproach, blame. ¶ galla, s. m. 1. A flock, a herd, drove. گله بان galla-bān, s. m. Pastor, shepherd, herdsman. گله باني galla-bān-ī, s. f. Keeping of a flock. 2. (Hind.) A vessel in which shopkeepers put money received for things sold in the course of the day, a till.
subs: s.f. Bringing forward (or making) a complaint; — gila-guẕārī karnā, To make a complaint.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گِلَه (gila).

def: s.m. A covering, an envelope, a wrapper, a case, cover, sheath, scabbard; a pillow-case; a quilt; — a pellicle; enclosing membrane; calyx (of a flower); the prepuce
more: n. m. 1. A case; pillow-case; sheath; covering. 2. A quilt ( sauṛ ). 3. The prepuce ( ghūṅgaṭ ). galāfnā, galefnā , v. a. See pāgnā. Bālūshāhī galef lo!
(from غلف Heb. To cover, to sheath) s. m. 1. A case, a cover, a sheath. 2. The prepuce.

def: s.m. A kind of box or receptacle for pān leaves; or for flakes of betel-leaf.
n. m. A case for gilaurī , q. v.

def: n. f. S. कालका 1. A squirrel. 1. Gilahrī kā peṛ ṭhikānā . Prov. The squirrel's home is on the tree. 2. Khedī gillī ant ko peṛ nīche ātī hai . Prov. The hunted squirrel runs to the tree. (The wanderer returns home at last). 3. Tumhārī gillo peṛā māṅgtī hai! Double entendre. 4. Karūṅgī dhūm se shādī, buā! nisbat to ṭhairī hai, Gilahrā hai merā, aur maṅjhlī bhābī kī gilahrī hai . Wom. Jān Sāhab. 2. ( Met .) An endearing term applied to a young child. gilahrī-mār . Squirrel-killer; field kite; the dwarf eagle; aquila pennata ; rat-killer ( chuhemār ).
s.f. (dialec.) 1˚ = gilahrī; — 2˚ = gillī, qq.v.; — adj. = gilī, q.v. s.f. = A گلہرا गिलहरा gilahrā, s.m. A squirrel
subs: s.m.f 'Squirrel-killer'; the field-kite; the dwarf-eagle, Arpula pennata.

def: s.m. = guṛać, q.v. s.m.(?) Name of a medicine, Menispermum glabrum or cordifolium (the infusion of which is used as a febrifuge).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گلو (gulō). Cognate with Sanskrit गल (gala, “throat, neck”). Doublet of گلا (galā).

def: s.m. The flower (or the pod) of the mahuʼā or Bassia latifolia (cf. guleṅdā).
more: n. m. The flower or pod of the mahuā ( Bassia latifolia ). Gīd gīd gilauṅdā khāë, ber ber mahue tale āë . Prov. Tame grown on the gīlaundā feeds, to the Mavvah now he constant speeds.
s. m. The flower of the mahūʼā (Bassia latifolia) after it has fallen off.
